close_reason: Added 4 CLI contract tests to devenv_cli.bats covering DEVENV_MEMORY, DEVENV_MEMORY_SWAP, DEVENV_CPUS overrides and default values. All 90 tests pass.
description: bin/devenv documents DEVENV_MEMORY, DEVENV_MEMORY_SWAP, and DEVENV_CPUS as user-facing override environment variables (shown in 'devenv help' output). These are used in cmd_start/start_container when constructing docker run arguments. There are no CLI contract tests verifying that setting these env vars passes the correct flags to docker (e.g., '--memory 4g' when DEVENV_MEMORY=4g). Per the testing spec, CLI contract tests should encode docker intent. Tests using the fake docker log should be added to devenv_cli.bats.
title: No tests for DEVENV_MEMORY / DEVENV_MEMORY_SWAP / DEVENV_CPUS resource override env vars

close_reason: Created tests/fixtures/bin/docker fake executable
description: Executable bash script that logs arguments to DOCKER_LOG or BATS_TMPDIR/docker_calls.log. Returns exit 0 by default. Supports FAKE_DOCKER_EXIT (override exit code) and FAKE_DOCKER_OUTPUT (canned stdout) env vars. Must handle subcommands: ps, stop, run, exec, build, info, images, volume, system. Start minimal, extend as tests require. Required by all CLI contract tests.
title: Create fake docker fixture: tests/fixtures/bin/docker

close_reason: Test complete
description: Verifying entrypoint fix works
title: Entrypoint cleanup test

close_reason: Added --default-toolchain stable to shared/tools/Dockerfile.cargo, aligning with spec. Committed in fa3fd1d.
description: The spec (devenv-architecture.md, cargo section) specifies the install command must include '--default-toolchain stable'. The inline tool_cargo stage in Dockerfile.devenv (line 33) includes this flag, but shared/tools/Dockerfile.cargo does not. The standalone tool image therefore installs rustup without pinning to stable toolchain, which may result in nightly or beta being selected. Fix: add '--default-toolchain stable' to the rustup install command in shared/tools/Dockerfile.cargo.
title: shared/tools/Dockerfile.cargo missing --default-toolchain stable flag

close_reason: Fixed: updated Testing Trophy URL in specs/testing-standard.md line 162 to https://kentcdodds.com/blog/the-testing-trophy-and-testing-classifications
description: specs/testing-standard.md line 152 links the 'Testing Trophy' reference to 'https://kentcdodds.com/blog/common-mistakes-with-react-testing-library' — this is the 'Common Mistakes with React Testing Library' article, NOT the Testing Trophy article. The correct URL for the Testing Trophy is https://kentcdodds.com/blog/the-testing-trophy-and-testing-classifications
title: Testing spec references wrong Testing Trophy blog post URL

close_reason: Removed redundant COPY --from=tool_fnm and orphaned FROM tools-fnm stage. fnm is already present via tool_node. All 99 tests pass.
description: In docker/devenv/Dockerfile.devenv, line 237 copies fnm from tool_fnm and line 242 copies fnm from tool_node. Line 237 is redundant and will be overwritten. Remove the COPY --from=tool_fnm line (237) since fnm is already included via tool_node which depends on it. This will be addressed when the inline stages are replaced with references to pre-built tool images (devenv-f81), but it is a separate correctness issue in the current file.
title: Dockerfile.devenv final stage has duplicate fnm COPY (lines 237 and 242)

The duplication HAS been resolved — derive_project_image_suffix now lives in shared/bash/primitives.sh and both bin/devenv and bin/build-devenv source it. The spec text is now stale and misleading. description: bats-core is not installed. Add it to the devenv Dockerfile (via shared/tools/ or docker/devenv/) so it is always available in containers. This is the prerequisite for all test execution. See research Open Decision #1 for installation method options.
title: Install bats-core in devenv environment

close_reason: Inline tool_beads stage removed from Dockerfile.devenv; replaced with FROM tools-beads:latest AS tool_beads, consistent with tool_tree_sitter pattern.
description: Move the inline tool_beads stage from docker/devenv/Dockerfile.devenv to a dedicated shared/tools/Dockerfile.beads file.
title: Extract tool_beads to shared/tools/Dockerfile.beads

close_reason: Fixed: replaced bare python3 with uv run python3 in allocate_port(); shellcheck clean; committed f4556c8
description: bin/devenv:167-172 — allocate_port() calls bare 'python3' but python3 is not installed in the devenv container. The function always returns 1, causing SSH port allocation to silently fail and fall through to die. Project convention is to use 'uv run python3' for anything Python-related. Recommended fix: rewrite to use 'uv run python3 -c ...' per research Open Decision #6.
title: Bug: allocate_port calls bare python3 which is not installed

close_reason: Added Makefile with test and lint targets; added .github/workflows/ci.yml running both on push and PR
description: The testing spec requires tests to be fast and runnable without Docker, but there is no Makefile, no 'make test' target, and no .github/workflows/ CI pipeline. Tests must be invoked manually with 'bats tests/bats/'. This means tests are never run automatically on commits/PRs, which defeats the purpose of having them. A Makefile with at minimum a 'test' target and ideally a CI workflow should be added.
title: No Makefile or CI pipeline for running tests

close_reason: Fixed: added cp /root/.fzf/bin/fzf /usr/local/bin/fzf in Dockerfile.fzf and updated Dockerfile.devenv COPY to use /usr/local/bin/fzf
description: shared/tools/Dockerfile.fzf installs fzf to /root/.fzf/bin/fzf and adds that to PATH via ENV, but does not copy/move the binary to /usr/local/bin/fzf. This means: (1) standalone tool image (build-devenv --tool fzf) does not expose fzf on the standard system PATH without the ENV being sourced, and (2) Dockerfile.devenv must COPY --from=tool_fzf /root/.fzf/bin/fzf rather than from /usr/local/bin/fzf, which is inconsistent with all other tools. Fix: add 'RUN cp /root/.fzf/bin/fzf /usr/local/bin/fzf && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/fzf' to Dockerfile.fzf and update the COPY in Dockerfile.devenv final stage accordingly.
title: shared/tools/Dockerfile.fzf does not move binary to /usr/local/bin

close_reason: Full testing infrastructure implemented: bats installed, 5 test files, 2 helpers, fake docker fixture, e2e placeholder, shellcheck clean, 74 tests pass without Docker
description: Epic: implement the full testing infrastructure per specs/testing-standard.md. Zero test infrastructure exists today. This covers bats-core installation, test helpers, fake docker fixtures, primitive tests, CLI contract tests, E2E placeholders, and verification. See research/testing-standard/research.md for the full gap analysis (26 gaps, 2 bugs, 6 open decisions).
title: Implement Testing Standard

close_reason: Added bats (and also beads, dolt, fzf) to README Available Tools section and architecture tree
description: README.md has an 'Available Tools' section listing tools bundled in the devenv image, but bats-core is not listed despite being installed and critical for the project's testing workflow. This creates a discoverability gap for developers new to the repo.
title: bats not listed in README Available Tools section

close_reason: Added explicit fallback mv and test verification for /usr/local/bin/bd and /usr/local/bin/beads in both shared/tools/Dockerfile.beads and the inline tool_beads stage in Dockerfile.devenv
description: shared/tools/Dockerfile.beads runs the beads install script without verifying or moving the binaries to /usr/local/bin/. Dockerfile.devenv final stage (lines 258-259) does COPY --from=tool_beads /usr/local/bin/bd and COPY --from=tool_beads /usr/local/bin/beads. If the beads installer places binaries elsewhere (e.g. /root/.local/bin/ or /usr/bin/), both COPYs will fail silently or error. Fix: after the install script, explicitly move/verify both 'bd' and 'beads' binaries are at /usr/local/bin/.
title: shared/tools/Dockerfile.beads does not guarantee binaries at /usr/local/bin/bd and /usr/local/bin/beads

close_reason: Added conditional mv/verify after installer in both Dockerfile.copilot-cli and Dockerfile.devenv inline stage. Handles /root/.local/bin/copilot fallback and fails hard if binary not found at either path.
description: shared/tools/Dockerfile.copilot-cli runs 'curl -fsSL https://gh.io/copilot-install | bash' but does not explicitly move or verify the binary ends up at /usr/local/bin/copilot. Dockerfile.devenv final stage (line 252) does COPY --from=tool_copilot-cli /usr/local/bin/copilot, which may fail if the installer places the binary elsewhere. Fix: after the install script, add an explicit check or mv to ensure the binary is at /usr/local/bin/copilot.
title: shared/tools/Dockerfile.copilot-cli does not guarantee binary at /usr/local/bin/copilot

close_reason: Split into two explicit tests: one asserting valid numeric port when uv is present, one asserting exit code 1 and empty stdout when uv is absent via restricted PATH.
description: tests/bats/devenv_primitives.bats: 'allocate_port: returns a numeric port between 1 and 65535' silently accepts exit code 1 with the comment 'If uv is not available, exit code 1 is acceptable'. This means the test passes vacuously when uv is absent, and the error-path behavior of allocate_port (returning 1 with no output) is never explicitly asserted. Two separate tests should exist: one asserting a valid numeric port when uv IS available (uv is present in the devenv container), and one asserting exit code 1 and empty stdout when uv is absent (using a PATH that excludes uv).
title: allocate_port test silently passes when uv is unavailable (exit-1 path untested)

close_reason: Removed broken docs/QUICKSTART.md reference from AGENTS.md line 94; kept only README.md reference
description: AGENTS.md:94 references 'docs/QUICKSTART.md' which does not exist. Either create the file with bd quick-start content, or remove the reference and point only to README.md.
title: Fix broken docs/QUICKSTART.md reference in AGENTS.md

close_reason: Audited all shared/tools/ Dockerfiles. Fixed 3 divergences: (1) Dockerfile.fnm missing unzip prerequisite for fnm installer; (2) Dockerfile.fzf leftover ENV PATH for /root/.fzf/bin after binary was moved to /usr/local/bin; (3) Dockerfile.bats unquoted ${BATS_VERSION#v} path expressions. Cargo --default-toolchain stable and fzf binary path were already fixed in prior commits.
description: The inline tool stages (e.g. tool_cargo, tool_fnm, tool_node, tool_fzf etc.) in docker/devenv/Dockerfile.devenv have drifted from their corresponding shared/tools/Dockerfile.* counterparts. Notable divergences: (1) tool_cargo inline has '--default-toolchain stable' but Dockerfile.cargo does not; (2) tool_fnm inline installs unzip first but Dockerfile.fnm does not; (3) tool_opencode inline has fallback logic Dockerfile.opencode lacks; (4) fzf binary source path differs. Until devenv-f81 (replace inline stages with pre-built image references) is resolved, both the inline stages and standalone Dockerfiles must be kept in sync. Consider this a tracking issue to audit all divergences.
title: Dockerfile.devenv inline tool stages diverge from shared/tools/ Dockerfiles

close_reason: Fake docker fixture already had per-subcommand output/exit support via FAKE_DOCKER_<SUBCMD>_OUTPUT and FAKE_DOCKER_<SUBCMD>_EXIT env vars. Removed the only remaining mktemp workaround in devenv_cli.bats ('volume rm --all with in-use volume' test) and replaced it with FAKE_DOCKER_VOLUME_LS_OUTPUT + FAKE_DOCKER_PS_OUTPUT env vars. All 90 tests pass.
description: tests/fixtures/bin/docker is a single fake that returns FAKE_DOCKER_OUTPUT for ALL docker calls, regardless of subcommand. This makes it impossible to write tests where different docker subcommands (e.g., 'docker volume ls', 'docker ps', 'docker volume inspect') need to return different output. The existing workaround (inline custom docker script via mktemp -d) is used in only one test (volume rm --all with in-use volume) but is duplicated effort. The fake docker should support per-subcommand output configuration, e.g., via environment variables like FAKE_DOCKER_VOLUME_LS_OUTPUT, FAKE_DOCKER_PS_OUTPUT.
title: Weak fake docker fixture cannot vary behavior per subcommand

close_reason: Added 3 CLI contract tests: devenv <path> verifies docker run, --port 2222 verifies port flag, re-attach verifies docker exec without docker run. Also extended fake docker fixture with IMAGES/RUN/EXEC per-subcommand overrides.
description: The testing spec lists CLI contract tests as required for bin/devenv, but the start path (cmd_start) and attach path (attach_container) have zero CLI contract tests in devenv_cli.bats. The --port flag is also untested. cmd_start is the default command (invoked when a path is given) and is the most user-facing code path. At minimum: 'devenv .' should be tested with fake docker to verify docker run is called with expected flags; '--port 2222 .' should verify the SSH port flag is passed; and a re-attach to an already-running container should be tested.
title: No CLI contract tests for devenv start / attach / --port flag

close_reason: Replaced inline tool_dolt (FROM repo-base + curl install) and tool_bats (FROM repo-base + bats-core install) stages with FROM tools-dolt:latest and FROM tools-bats:latest references. tool_tree_sitter and tool_beads were already referencing pre-built images (FROM tools-tree-sitter:latest, FROM tools-beads:latest). All 99 tests pass. Committed 9e64c5b.
description: After all four tool Dockerfiles are extracted (tree-sitter, beads, dolt, bats), update docker/devenv/Dockerfile.devenv to remove the inline stage definitions and instead use COPY --from= referencing the separately-built images.
title: Update Dockerfile.devenv to reference extracted tool Dockerfiles

The comment above the second definition ('Build the repo-base image') belongs to build_stage_repo_base(), indicating a copy-paste mistake. Per spec section 4: test each copy independently 'until the duplication is resolved by extracting a shared library.' This task extracts both functions to a shared bash library (e.g., shared/bash/primitives.sh) and updates both scripts to source it.
title: Extract duplicated primitives to shared library

close_reason: Replaced 4 raw [[ ${output} =~ ... ]] pattern matches with assert_stdout_contains in devenv_cli.bats. All 90 tests pass.
description: Several tests in devenv_cli.bats check error output using raw bash: '[[ "${output}" =~ "..."]]. This bypasses the assert helpers defined in tests/helpers/assert_output.bash and produces less readable failure messages. Per the testing standard's assertion about reducing boilerplate with helpers, these should use assert_stderr_contains or assert_stdout_contains. Affected tests: 'devenv stop (no target)', 'devenv volume (no subcommand)', 'devenv volume rm (no args)', 'devenv volume rm --all with in-use volume'.
title: devenv_cli.bats uses raw bash pattern match instead of assert_stderr_contains for error messages

close_reason: Fixed: enhanced fake docker fixture with per-subcommand FAKE_DOCKER_<SUBCOMMAND>_OUTPUT/_EXIT env vars; rewrote the weak test to assert exit 0 and verify 'volume rm devenv-test-vol' in docker log. All 82 tests pass.
description: tests/bats/devenv_cli.bats test 'devenv volume rm --all --force: exits 0 and fake docker called volume rm' has a comment admitting it 'may not fully exercise the rm path' and asserts '[[ status -eq 0 || status -eq 1 ]]' — i.e., any exit code is acceptable. This is not a real test. The fix requires improving the fake docker fixture to return different output per subcommand so that 'docker volume ls' returns a volume name while subsequent calls (ps, inspect, rm) return appropriate responses.
title: devenv volume rm --all --force test has a weak assertion (accepts exit 0 or 1)

close_reason: Fixed in commit a1cab3b: hardened entrypoint.sh with unconditional state cleanup, two-pass dolt process kill, and final sql-server.info removal before server start
description: Stale sql-server.info files survived container restarts because clean_stale_state() used PID-liveness checks (kill -0) which are unreliable across restarts due to PID reuse. This caused 'database is locked' errors and database corruption. Fixed in commit a1cab3b by: (1) removing all state files unconditionally at boot, (2) two-pass kill of ALL dolt sql-server processes, (3) final rm -f of sql-server.info before starting the new server. Also updated bd hooks to 0.58.0 and docs.
title: Fix: harden entrypoint dolt lifecycle to prevent database lock errors

close_reason: shellcheck passes with zero warnings on all production scripts and test helpers
description: Run shellcheck on bin/devenv, bin/build-devenv, scripts/install-devenv, shared/bash/log.sh. Confirm zero warnings. This satisfies the static analysis tier of the testing trophy. shellcheck v0.11.0 is already installed. Consider adding a shellcheck runner script or Makefile target.
title: Run shellcheck on all scripts and confirm zero warnings

description: Install and configure bun (JavaScript runtime/package manager) as an available tool in the devenv container. Add it to the devenv configuration so it is available in the development environment.

close_reason: Added conditional mv logic to shared/tools/Dockerfile.opencode to ensure binary is always at /usr/local/bin/opencode, matching the inline stage in Dockerfile.devenv. Added explicit error if neither install path is found.
description: shared/tools/Dockerfile.opencode runs 'curl -fsSL https://opencode.ai/install | bash' but does not explicitly verify or move the binary to /usr/local/bin/opencode. The inline stage in Dockerfile.devenv (lines 132-138) has conditional logic to handle both /root/.opencode/bin/opencode and /usr/local/bin/opencode install paths. The standalone Dockerfile.opencode is missing this robustness. Dockerfile.devenv final stage at line 251 copies COPY --from=tool_opencode /usr/local/bin/opencode which will fail if the installer put it in /root/.opencode/bin/. Fix: replicate the conditional mv logic in Dockerfile.opencode so the binary is always at /usr/local/bin/opencode.
title: shared/tools/Dockerfile.opencode does not guarantee binary at /usr/local/bin/opencode

title: Extract inline tool stages to shared/tools/ Dockerfiles

close_reason: Updated specs/devenv-architecture.md:524-527 to show 'uv run python3' instead of bare 'python3', matching the implementation in bin/devenv:140-145 which was intentionally fixed in devenv-71n.
description: bin/devenv:133-137 uses 'uv run python3' for port allocation but specs/devenv-architecture.md:525-526 shows plain 'python3'. Either remove the uv wrapper in the implementation or update the spec to document the uv dependency explicitly.
title: Resolve allocate_port uv dependency vs spec

close_reason: Created tests/bats/devenv_cli.bats — 11 tests, all passing
description: CLI contract tests for bin/devenv. Runs devenv as executable with fake docker on PATH. Prerequisite: fake docker must handle 'docker info' (validate_docker). Test scenarios: devenv list, stop --all, stop (no target), volume rm (no args), volume rm --all with in-use volume, volume (no subcommand), volume list, volume rm --force, volume rm with confirmation, help. 10 scenarios per research.
title: Create tests/bats/devenv_cli.bats
